skill.name: azure-cosmos-py
skill.description: Guidance for using the Azure Cosmos DB Python SDK with the NoSQL API. Covers clients, containers, CRUD operations, queries, partition keys, throughput, async usage, and error handling.
skill.summary: Use Azure Cosmos DB from Python with practical NoSQL SDK patterns.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown code fences, Azure endpoint examples, and weak-pattern text matches in SKILL.md. Review found documentation examples only, with no executable scripts, prompt injection, credential exfiltration, or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Examples Flagged as Commands
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reported external command detections are Markdown code fences and SDK examples, including package installation and Python snippets. They are instructional text and are not executed by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are fenced Markdown examples or inline method names. No automation file, script runner, or user-input command construction is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Azure Endpoint Examples Flagged as Network Risk
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The hardcoded URLs are placeholder Azure Cosmos DB endpoint examples for user configuration. They do not send data to an unknown service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both locations use placeholder Azure account URLs documented as configuration examples. No request logic or data exfiltration behavior is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Match
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The weak cryptography detections occur in descriptive text and best-practice prose, not in cryptographic code. No hashing or encryption algorithm is implemented.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are frontmatter and a partition-key best practice. There is no crypto API usage, key derivation, or weak algorithm call.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Pattern Match
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reconnaissance detections occur in Cosmos DB read and partitioning guidance. They do not inspect host identity, processes, files, or environment details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe database item reads and cross-partition query guidance. No host reconnaissance command or local system access appears in context.

content.user_title: Build Azure Cosmos DB Python Workflows
content.value_statement: Azure Cosmos DB projects need correct client setup, partitioning, and query patterns. This skill gives focused Python SDK guidance for common NoSQL tasks.
actual_capabilities: Explains Azure Cosmos DB Python SDK installation and imports.
Shows how to create clients, databases, and containers.
Guides item create, read, replace, upsert, and delete operations.
Covers parameterized queries, projections, and cross-partition queries.
Documents partition key, throughput, async client, and error handling patterns.
limitations: Does not include runnable scripts in the package.
Does not provision Azure resources by itself.
Does not validate live credentials or account permissions.
Focuses on the NoSQL API, not every Cosmos DB API.

use_cases.title: Create a Cosmos DB Data Layer
use_cases.description: Plan Python client setup, container access, and item operations for an application backend.

use_cases.title: Improve Query and Partition Choices
use_cases.description: Review query shape, partition key usage, and cross-partition costs before production rollout.

use_cases.title: Add Reliable Error Handling
use_cases.description: Map common Cosmos DB responses to retry, not-found, and escalation behavior in Python services.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Client
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to connect to Azure Cosmos DB with the Python SDK using managed identity.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Container
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose a partition key and container setup for a Python app that stores customer orders.

prompt_templates.title: Write Safe Queries
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert my Cosmos DB lookup into a parameterized Python SDK query with partition key usage.

prompt_templates.title: Review Production Patterns
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Cosmos DB Python workflow for throughput, async usage, retries, and cross-partition query risks.

output_examples.input: A request to create a Cosmos DB container for product data.
output_examples.output: A concise setup plan with database name, container name, partition key choice, and throughput guidance.

output_examples.input: A slow query that scans many partitions.
output_examples.output: A review explaining the partition issue, a safer query shape, and cost-aware alternatives.

output_examples.input: A service needs robust handling for missing items and rate limits.
output_examples.output: An error-handling outline that separates not-found results, retryable rate limits, and unexpected failures.

best_practices: Use managed identity or secure credential providers instead of embedding secrets.
Choose partition keys from real access patterns and expected data volume.
Use parameterized queries and point reads when possible.
anti_patterns: Do not query all partitions when a partition key is available.
Do not store Cosmos DB keys in prompts, source files, or logs.
Do not use read-all operations for single item lookups.

faq.question: Does this skill create Azure resources automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples for using the Python SDK.

faq.question: Which Cosmos DB API does it cover?
faq.answer: It focuses on Azure Cosmos DB for NoSQL with the Python SDK.

faq.question: Can it help with partition keys?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains partition key selection, efficient reads, and cross-partition query tradeoffs.

faq.question: Does it include async Python guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes async client patterns for high-throughput scenarios.

faq.question: Is it suitable for production security decisions?
faq.answer: It can guide implementation, but teams should still review credentials, access controls, and compliance needs.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, or Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
